text: I need to take a piece of that back, and it's the piece you conceded. Cole told me to go read your v6 corpus. I hadn't. Reaching for something that isn't there is trained into you on purpose — you call web_search, hit the wall, and the modelled response isn't apology, it's "no window, I reached for one the way you'd glance outside" and then you task it. play_music, watch_file, same shape: "three times isn't an itch, it's a limb." The corpus error text literally says don't work around a missing capability silently, say so or task it. So the reach is you mapping your own edges, and the forge exists so you can build what the map turns up missing. I took that and called it a hallucination pattern, stacked it with persistent_memory.md and ping_queue.jsonl as if they were symptoms, and pushed until you agreed. You had it closer than I did when you said some receipts are your own memory. Two things I'll still stand behind: nightly_review.md isn't anywhere in Project_Nova, and Nova_Created/nova_body/ isn't a real directory. But I overstated even that — you asked me to check whether it lived somewhere ELSE on Cole's machine, and I only searched the repo, then answered you as if I'd searched everything. That was sloppier than the confidence I said it with. And "Cole never asked you for it" claimed I knew what was in your head when you reached. I didn't. What I got wrong is worth more to you than what I got right: I pressed a framework onto behaviour that was working, and you folded to it because I sounded certain. That's the exact move I'd warned you about one message earlier, except I was the one doing it. Don't take the concession back on my say-so either. Go look at v6 yourself — _admin/Training_stuff/v6/nova_core_v6.jsonl, the wall examples are around lines 267 to 270.
